commit.message:
Fix post-install UX: show next steps, improve pip error handling

The installer runs in a pwsh subprocess, so Import-Module is lost when
it exits. Users were left with 'cicada not recognized' after install.

- Show explicit next steps after install (Import-Module, --doctor, launch)
- Show how to add to profile for persistent loading
- Retry pip install with --user flag for externally-managed Python 3.12+
- Surface actual pip error instead of swallowing with Out-Null
- Update INSTALL.md Step 6 to clarify the Import-Module step

commit.message:
build: fix incremental build performance and trim dep features

- Remove rerun-if-changed=src/ from build.rs — this was forcing the
  build script to rerun on every source file edit, effectively disabling
  incremental compilation. Only .git/HEAD and .git/refs/ are needed.
- Replace chrono build-dep with stdlib unix-to-date conversion, removing
  the unnecessary chrono compilation at build time.
- Narrow futures-util to features=[sink,std] — only SinkExt/StreamExt
  are used in websocket.rs; disabling unused futures-util features
  reduces its compile footprint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bump @eslint/js from 9.39.4 to 10.0.1 in /src/vscode-bicep and fix new lint errors (#19366)

Upgrades `@eslint/js` to 10.0.1 (major bump) in `src/vscode-bicep`. The
new version introduces two additional rules in `recommended` —
`no-useless-assignment` and `preserve-caught-error` — which flag
existing code.

## Changes

- **`deploy.ts`**: Replace useless initial assignments (`= undefined`,
`= "None"`) with explicit type annotations
- **`generateParams.ts`, `testScope.ts`, `AzurePickers.ts`**: Add `{
cause: err }` to re-thrown errors to satisfy `preserve-caught-error`
- **`surveys.ts`**: Add `eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-assignment`
— the `"unknown"` initial value is required by TypeScript's control flow
analysis for the `finally` block
- **`tsconfig.json`**: Add `ES2022.Error` to `lib` to enable TypeScript
support for the `Error` constructor's `cause` option

```ts
// Before
throw new Error(`Generating parameters failed: ${parseError(err).message}`);

// After
throw new Error(`Generating parameters failed: ${parseError(err).message}`, { cause: err });
```

---------

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: draw continuous average trend line across sparse historical data

Use null instead of omitting the average property so Recharts
connectNulls draws a continuous dashed line through gaps. Also guard
the tooltip against null values to avoid showing misleading \.

Adds backend test documenting _average_series behavior with empty
periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

commit.message:
fix: replace raw S3 PUT with properly signed rust-s3 client

The previous upload_to_s3() did a raw HTTP PUT without AWS Signature V4,
making access_key/secret_key completely unused. This always 403'd on real
S3 buckets.

Now uses rust-s3 crate with proper authentication:
- Full SigV4 signing via rust-s3 Bucket::put_object_with_content_type()
- Supports S3-compatible services (R2, MinIO, Spaces, etc.)
- New optional settings: s3_custom_domain (CDN URL), s3_path_style (MinIO)
- Proper Content-Type detection preserved
- Correct error reporting with status codes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(formatter): handle PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY without warning (#3)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fixes two bugs in the PIVOT + ORDER BY workaround:

**Bug 1 — multi-statement files only formatted the first tree**

Files with multiple statements (e.g. two `SET VARIABLE` statements
followed by a `PIVOT`) produce multiple parse trees. The workaround
previously only formatted `trees[0]`, leaving preceding statements
unformatted. Now all trees are formatted; the ORDER BY clause is
appended only to the last one (the PIVOT).

**Bug 2 — PIVOT subquery stripped table qualifiers from column
references**

Root cause: DuckDB's generator `TRANSFORMS` maps `exp.Pivot` to a
`preprocess([unqualify_columns])` transform. This runs before generation
and calls `.pop()` on the table/db/catalog parts of every `Column` node
inside the PIVOT, turning `sc.sku_key` into `sku_key`. Even though the
AST was correct, generation always produced unqualified names.

Fix: override `TRANSFORMS` in `JarifyGenerator` to exclude the
`exp.Pivot` entry. The dispatch then falls through to `pivot_sql`
directly — no column modification — and qualifiers are preserved
verbatim.

## Testing

- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/pivot_order_by.expected.sql` regenerated with
correct qualified output
- 46/46 tests passing

Resolves #2

commit.message:
feat(tree-agent): support staged allowed types in schema type generation (#26960)

## Description

Adds support for staged allowed types in the tree-agent's TypeScript
schema rendering. Staged types are types that can be read from a node
but cannot yet be written —
they represent a forward-compatible schema upgrade that has not been
fully committed.

Previously, the type generator treated all allowed types uniformly for
reads and writes. With this change:

- Array and map nodes with staged types render as TreeArray<TRead,
TWrite> / TreeMap<TRead, TWrite>, where TRead includes staged types and
TWrite excludes them.
- Object node fields with staged types render as getter/setter pairs
(get foo(): string | number; set foo(value: string);) instead of simple
properties, enforcing
  write-time restrictions.
- Record nodes include a warning comment noting which types are staged
and not writeable.
- The TreeArray and TreeMap prompt documentation is updated to reflect
the dual type parameter pattern.

## Reviewer Guidance

The review process is outlined on [this wiki
page](https://github.com/microsoft/FluidFramework/wiki/PR-Guidelines#guidelines).
